subject: Geography
title: The Robinson Projection
description: A quick win: understand The Robinson Projection.

hook.text: Arthur Robinson designed a projection that doesn't preserve anything perfectly—on purpose.

buildup.text: It compromises on area, shape, and distance so that nothing looks too distorted.

discovery.text: National Geographic adopted it in 1988 as its standard world map.

twist.text: Robinson created it by tweaking numbers until the map 'looked right' to him—it was subjective.

climax.text: Sometimes the best map is the one that feels honest, even if nothing is mathematically exact.

punchline.text: The best world map was designed by eye, not by math.

quiz.question: Why did National Geographic choose the Robinson projection?
options: It minimizes all distortions without eliminating any
